meanings.definition: a social class comprising those who do manual labor or work for wages
examples: there is a shortage of skilled labor in this field

meanings.definition: concluding state of pregnancy; from the onset of contractions to the birth of a child
examples: she was in labor for six hours

meanings.definition: a political party formed in Great Britain in 1900; characterized by the promotion of labor's interests and formerly the socialization of key industries
productive work (especially physical work done for wages)
examples: his labor did not require a great deal of skill
She was digging away at her math homework

meanings.definition: strive and make an effort to reach a goal
examples: She tugged for years to make a decent living
We have to push a little to make the deadline!
She is driving away at her doctoral thesis
